{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks for the most appropriate statistical test to determine the statistical significance of the difference in heights of school children among three socio-economic groups. The Student's t-test (A) is used for comparing the means of two groups, making it unsuitable for comparing three groups. The Chi-squared test (B) is used for categorical data, not continuous data like height. The Paired t-test (C) is used for comparing the means of the same group measured at two different times or conditions, which is not applicable here. The One-way Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) (D) is used to compare the means of three or more groups, making it the most appropriate test for this scenario.", "answer_choice": "D"}
